content.text:
<user_query>
When I collapse the sidebar the icon jumps to the right before sliding into place. This jump is very jarring. Instead it should stay in the same position stuck to the left the entire time.
</user_query>
I'm looking into what's causing the sidebar collapse animation to not work properly.

I'm looking at the sidebar collapse behavior—the icon is jumping to the right before settling into place. The grid layout shifts from a two-column setup with flexible spacing when expanded to a single-column layout when collapsed, and I need to figure out why the icon is moving unexpectedly during this transition.

I found the issue in `SidebarMenuButton` — when the sidebar is expanded, the `justify-center` class isn't applied, so it falls back to the parent's grid layout, but the user's custom classes with `grid` are overriding the flex defaults and causing the alignment problem.

Looking at the padding and grid dimensions: when collapsed at 48px width, the button's content area (48 - 16px padding) is exactly 32px, which matches the 2rem grid column, leaving no space for centering. When expanded, the grid template switches from `2rem minmax(0,1fr)` to just `2rem`, and the transition between these templates is causing the visual glitch during the collapse animation. The dropdown content is also affected by this grid recalculation.

The real issue is that when `collapsed` becomes true, the grid immediately applies `justify-center`, which centers the 2rem icon column within the full 256px button width before the sidebar's CSS transition kicks in. This creates that rightward jump—the icon moves to the center of the wide button. Then as the sidebar shrinks from 256px to 48px, the centered column moves left with it, creating the sliding effect.

The user's fix using `justify-items-start` for the collapsed state should keep the icon anchored left, but the library's SidebarMenuButton component is likely overriding this with its own `justify-center` class.

Since the user's className gets applied last in the class merging logic, adding `justify-start` should override the library's `justify-center`—assuming the utility class merging handles semantic conflicts correctly.

I found the cause. The `SidebarMenuButton` in the shared UI package applies `justify-center` when collapsed:

```285:287:packages/ui/src/components/ui/sidebar.tsx
        menuButtonVariants({ size, variant }),
        collapsed ? "justify-center px-2" : "",
```

On your button, that sets `justify-content: center` on the grid. The moment you click collapse:

1. `collapsed` flips to `true`, so the library immediately slaps `justify-center` on the button.
2. But the sidebar width is still `256px` (it's the one thing with the width transition on it).
3. So the single `2rem` grid track gets centered inside the still-wide button — the icon snaps to the right.
4. As the sidebar width transitions from `256px` → `48px`, the centered track slides back to the left.

Your own `justify-items-start` in [apps/org-next/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx](apps/org-next/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx) doesn't help because that's a different CSS property (`justify-items`, not `justify-content`), so twMerge doesn't strip `justify-center`.

The fix is to keep `justify-content: flex-start` throughout, so the `2rem` track stays pinned to the left edge regardless of the wrapping button's current width.

Two places this could be fixed — I want to confirm scope before proposing the plan.

 Good, so `twMerge` means the user can override `justify-center` with `justify-start` in their className since the last one wins. Let me look more closely at the actual issue—checking the SidebarHeader padding, which uses `px-1 pt-2` when collapsed and `px-2 py-2` when expanded, while the user's AppSidebar has matching `px-2 py-2` on an inner div. That should be fine, but let me trace through the user's code more carefully.

The SidebarHeader gets `p-0 transition-none`, which overrides the library's padding entirely. Inside that is a div with `px-2 py-2` (8px padding on each side). The button inside uses `menuButtonVariants` with size=md, giving it `h-10 gap-2 px-2 py-2`. When expanded, the sidebar is 256px wide, so the inner content is 240px after the 16px padding. When collapsed though, the sidebar is only 48px, but the user's inner div still has `px-2 py-2` (16px total padding), leaving just 32px for content—yet the button's grid column is set to 2rem (32px), which exactly fills that space. But wait, the user's override with `p-0` means the SidebarHeader itself has no padding, so the inner div's padding is all that matters. That's actually the constraint here. The issue is that `justify-items-start` and `justify-center` operate on different CSS properties—`justify-items` vs `justify-content`—so they don't conflict in tailwind-merge's grouping system. The user's `justify-items-start` won't actually override the library's `justify-center`, which is why the grid column keeps centering during the collapse transition. I need to add `justify-start` to the className instead, which targets `justify-content` directly and will properly override the library's centering behavior. The fix is to add `justify-start` to override the library's `justify-center`, which will keep the icon left-aligned throughout the collapse animation instead of jumping right first. Since the user's className gets merged last with `cn()` and `twMerge`, this override will take precedence and solve the jarring motion.

questions.prompt: Where should we fix the jump?

options.label: Only in apps/org-next's AppSidebar (add `justify-start` to the button className to override the library's `justify-center`).
In the shared `@workspace/ui` Sidebar component (make `SidebarMenuButton` not force `justify-center` when a grid/custom layout is used, e.g. drop `justify-center` or anchor it to the start).
Both - fix the library default to `justify-start` during/across collapse AND keep the app override as a safety net.

input.name: fix sidebar icon jump
input.overview: Pin the tenant avatar to the left edge throughout the sidebar collapse transition by overriding the library's `justify-center` on the `SidebarMenuButton` inside `AppSidebar`.
input.plan:
## Root cause

`SidebarMenuButton` in [packages/ui/src/components/ui/sidebar.tsx](packages/ui/src/components/ui/sidebar.tsx) adds `justify-center` whenever `collapsed` is true:

```285:287:packages/ui/src/components/ui/sidebar.tsx
        menuButtonVariants({ size, variant }),
        collapsed ? "justify-center px-2" : "",
```

On our grid button that becomes `justify-content: center`. When you click collapse:

- `collapsed` flips true instantly, so the button gets `justify-content: center` right now.
- The sidebar width is the only element with the width transition, so it is still ~256px for the first frame.
- The single `2rem` grid column gets centered in the still-wide button — the avatar visually snaps to the right.
- As the sidebar shrinks to 48px, the centered track slides back to the left, producing the "jump then slide" feel.

Our existing `justify-items-start` / `justify-items-stretch` do not fix this — `justify-items` targets items inside their cells, not the placement of the grid tracks (which is `justify-content`). So twMerge leaves `justify-center` in place.

## Fix

In [apps/org-next/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx](apps/org-next/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx), add `justify-start` to the `SidebarMenuButton` className so twMerge strips the library's `justify-center`. Since user-supplied classes are concatenated last via `cn(...)`, and `cn` uses `twMerge`, the later `justify-start` wins in the `justify-content` group.

Concretely, update the `className` passed to `SidebarMenuButton` (lines ~53–65) so the base line reads:

```tsx
"grid h-10 w-full items-center justify-start gap-2 text-sidebar-foreground shadow-none ring-0",
```

No other changes required:
- `col-start-1 justify-self-start` on the avatar already pins it to the first column.
- `grid-cols-[2rem]` (collapsed) / `grid-cols-[2rem_minmax(0,1fr)]` (expanded) and the existing `transition-[grid-template-columns]` keep the template animation smooth.
- With `justify-content: flex-start`, the 2rem column stays flush to the button's left padding for the whole width transition, so the avatar no longer jumps.

## Verification

- Collapse the sidebar and watch the avatar — it should remain anchored at the left and only the label/chevron should disappear while the sidebar shrinks.
- Expand the sidebar — avatar stays put, label grows in.
- Keyboard/screen reader behavior is unchanged (no structural edits).

todos.content: Add `justify-start` to the `SidebarMenuButton` className in `apps/org-next/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx` to override the library's `justify-center` during collapse.
Manually verify the collapse/expand animation keeps the avatar pinned to the left with no jump.

content.text:
<user_query>
also there is a bottom border when the sidebar is collapsed that shouldn't be there, add to the plan to remove it please
</user_query>

Looking at the code, there are two borders in [apps/org-next/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx](apps/org-next/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx):

- Line 43: `<div className="border-sidebar-border border-b px-2 py-2">` — a bottom border under the tenant switcher in the header.
- Lines 121–125: a `border-t` above the `SidebarTrigger` footer (top border).

The one that appears "at the bottom when collapsed" is almost certainly the header's `border-b` under the shrunken tenant switcher. I'll update the plan to hide it when collapsed.

 I need to track down where the bottom border is appearing in the sidebar. Looking at the AppSidebar component, I can see the header section has a `border-b` class that's creating that bottom border, and there's another border styling further down in the component that I need to examine more closely.

The issue is that when the sidebar collapses to just show the avatar, that bottom border under it looks odd. I should conditionally remove the `border-b` class when the sidebar is in its collapsed state.

## Verification

input.new_string:
## Hide collapsed bottom border

The tenant-switcher wrapper inside `SidebarHeader` currently always renders `border-b`:

```43:43:apps/org-next/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx
        <div className="border-sidebar-border border-b px-2 py-2">
```

When the sidebar collapses, that leaves a short horizontal rule under just the avatar, which looks out of place.

Update that line to conditionally drop the bottom border when `collapsed` is true, using the existing `collapsed` value from `useSidebar()` and `cn`:

```tsx
<div
  className={cn(
    "border-sidebar-border px-2 py-2",
    !collapsed && "border-b",
  )}
>
```

No other layout/padding changes — only the border visibility is tied to the collapsed state.

## Verification

- Collapse the sidebar and watch the avatar — it should remain anchored at the left and only the label/chevron should disappear while the sidebar shrinks.
- Confirm the horizontal rule under the tenant switcher disappears when collapsed and returns when expanded.
- Expand the sidebar — avatar stays put, label grows in, border reappears.
- Keyboard/screen reader behavior is unchanged (no structural edits).

- Extended the Verification section to include checking the border disappears when collapsed and returns when expanded.

Let me know if you want me to also kill the `border-t` above the `SidebarTrigger` footer when collapsed, or if that one should stay. Otherwise, say the word and I'll execute the plan.

<user_query>
can we do this without grid and use flex instead?
</user_query>

Good call — the grid was only there as a workaround for the library's `justify-center`. Once we override that with `justify-start`, plain flex achieves the same effect with less CSS.

 I need to convert this grid layout to flexbox while preserving the same behavior—the avatar stays fixed on the left, and the label/chevron wrapper expands to fill available space when expanded or collapses when the sidebar is minimized.

The key insight is that grid was originally used to prevent the avatar from centering when the library applies `justify-center`. With flexbox, I can achieve the same result by explicitly setting `justify-start` on the container, which overrides any centering from the parent. Then I'll use `shrink-0` on the avatar to keep it at a fixed width and `flex-1` on the label wrapper to let it expand when needed.

Building the flex version with proper styling... The flex approach handles the collapse smoothly without needing column animation—the avatar stays pinned to the left via `flex-start`, the label and chevron disappear based on the `collapsed` state, and the sidebar width transition creates the visual effect without any jumping. I could add opacity animation to the label for a smoother fade, but the core issue of icon jumping is solved just by preventing the library's `justify-center` override.

input.new_string:
## Fix — switch to flex The grid with `grid-cols-[2rem_...]` was only a workaround for the library's `justify-center`. With `justify-start` forcing the button's main-axis alignment to the start, plain flex is enough: the fixed-size avatar (`shrink-0`) always sits at the left edge, and the label/chevron sit next to it when expanded. In [apps/org-next/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx](apps/org-next/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx), rewrite the `SidebarMenuButton` (lines ~51–88) as: ```tsx
{tenantInitials}
{!collapsed ? (
{tenantLabel}
) : null} ``` Key points: - `flex ... justify-start` overrides the library's `justify-center` via `twMerge` (same class group), so the avatar stays anchored at the button's left padding throughout the sidebar width transition — no jump. - `shrink-0` on the avatar keeps it exactly `size-8` (2rem) even while the flex container shrinks during the sidebar's `width` transition 256 → 48. - The label/chevron wrapper uses `min-w-0 flex-1` so the text can truncate when expanded. Removing it when `collapsed` is true is visually fine because the sidebar width animates from `256 → 48`, so the avatar simply ends up alone against the left edge. There is no extra `grid-template-columns` animation to worry about. - The previous `transition-[grid-template-columns]` on the button is dropped; the only motion is the existing `width` transition on the `Sidebar` wrapper in the `@workspace/ui` package. - The explanatory comment about the grid workaround can be removed.

- Dropped `grid-cols-[...]`, `transition-[grid-template-columns]`, and the related explanatory comment.
- Kept the `border-b` conditional change and verification steps intact.

With the sidebar at `48px`, the padding chain (header inner `px-2` + button `px-2` from `menuButtonVariants`) leaves only `16px` of inner content box on the button, and the button's `overflow-hidden` (from the library's base variants) clips the `32px` avatar.

The cleanest fix that keeps the avatar at the same visual position throughout the transition (no new jump): override `overflow-hidden` with `overflow-visible` on the button when collapsed. I need to reduce padding so the avatar fits completely. Instead, I should adjust the inner div's padding based on the collapsed state to compensate and maintain a consistent 16px offset from the sidebar edge throughout the animation. I'm working through the padding calculations for each approach. Approach A keeps a small 8px shift, Approach B makes it worse at 16px, and Approach C tries to lock the avatar at a fixed 16px offset by using left-only padding on the button when collapsed—though that creates an asymmetric padding situation that feels awkward.

The real issue is that I'm overcomplicating this. The user's main frustration was the jarring jump-to-right-then-slide motion, not minor pixel shifts during the transition itself. The cleanest fix is to remove the horizontal padding from the inner div when collapsed, so the button can expand to fill the full 48px width and the avatar sits properly without clipping. This shifts the avatar 8px left during the collapse transition, which should look natural. I think `overflow-visible` is the cleanest approach since twMerge will handle the override properly, and the avatar will extend past the button boundary without clipping while the hover state still looks fine. But the simplest solution is just removing the inner div's padding when collapsed and keeping the button padding consistent—this gives a steady 16px offset for the avatar in expanded state and an 8px shift when collapsed, which feels acceptable for the interaction.

Actually, I should set the inner div to have no padding when collapsed and standard padding when expanded. The tricky part is that the `collapsed` state flips instantly when clicked, so the padding changes in one frame while the sidebar width animates over 300ms—meaning the avatar jumps 8px left immediately, then the sidebar shrinks around it. That's a bit jarring, but it's the simplest implementation.

To avoid any jump at all, I'd need to keep the avatar at a constant 16px offset from the sidebar edge throughout the transition. Setting up equations where each container's padding reduces the available space for the next layer, and the avatar needs to fit within the button's inner content area starting at that 16px mark.

A simpler approach might be to just remove the button's padding when collapsed and accept a small 8px shift during the transition—it's subtle enough that it won't feel jarring. Or I could shrink the avatar itself in collapsed mode to something like 24px, which would fit more cleanly within the button's bounds. A smooth 8px slide over 300ms won't feel jarring at all. Let me just add `overflow-visible` to the button in collapsed mode.

Actually, let me recalculate the dimensions to make sure I'm seeing this right. The sidebar is 48px wide, the inner div has 8px padding on each side leaving 32px for content. The button takes up that full 32px width with its own 8px padding on each side, so only 16px is visible of the 32px avatar—which matches what I'm seeing in the screenshot.

I have two solid options: either remove the button's padding when collapsed so the avatar fits fully inside, or remove the inner div's padding when collapsed to give the button more room. Both shift the avatar 8px left, but removing the inner div's padding feels cleaner since it keeps the button's own padding symmetric. Though actually, I'm wondering if I should just lean into the library's built-in SidebarHeader transitions instead of overriding them with `p-0 transition-none`. The library already handles a smooth padding transition from `px-2 py-2` to `px-1 pt-2` over 300ms, which should work fine without any jarring jumps.

Let me just go with the simplest fix: toggle the inner div's padding off when collapsed and disable the transition to avoid animation artifacts.

Actually, thinking through the motion more carefully—if padding changes instantly while the sidebar width animates over 300ms, the avatar will jump 8px to the left right away, then stay put as the sidebar shrinks. That's still a jump, which defeats the purpose. To keep the avatar perfectly still throughout, I should use overflow-visible on the button so it can extend beyond its bounds without repositioning, letting the avatar stay anchored to the sidebar edge the whole time. Looking at the screenshot more carefully, the avatar is getting clipped on the right side when the sidebar collapses. Adding `overflow-visible` to the button when collapsed should fix this—the avatar will extend beyond the sidebar edge without being cut off, and it'll stay in the same position throughout the expand/collapse cycle. For the expanded state, I'll keep `overflow-hidden` to handle any text truncation. So the fix is conditional: `collapsed && "overflow-visible"` in the className, and I can use `twMerge` to override the base `overflow-hidden` from `menuButtonVariants`.

todos.content: Prevent the avatar from being clipped in the collapsed sidebar by overriding overflow-hidden with overflow-visible on SidebarMenuButton when collapsed
Verify typecheck and biome still pass after the fix

input.old_string:
<SidebarMenuButton
                  aria-label={switcherAriaLabel}
                  className={cn(
                    "flex h-10 w-full items-center justify-start gap-2 text-sidebar-foreground shadow-none ring-0",
                    "hover:bg-sidebar-accent/80 data-[state=open]:bg-sidebar-accent/90",
                    "focus-visible:ring-0 focus-visible:ring-offset-0",
                    "dark:hover:bg-sidebar-accent/50",

input.new_string:
<SidebarMenuButton
                  aria-label={switcherAriaLabel}
                  className={cn(
                    "flex h-10 w-full items-center justify-start gap-2 text-sidebar-foreground shadow-none ring-0",
                    "hover:bg-sidebar-accent/80 data-[state=open]:bg-sidebar-accent/90",
                    "focus-visible:ring-0 focus-visible:ring-offset-0",
                    "dark:hover:bg-sidebar-accent/50",
                    /* In collapsed mode the sidebar (48px) is narrower than the
                       avatar plus the button's own px-2 padding, so the library's
                       overflow-hidden clips the avatar. Keep the avatar visible
                       without shifting its left position. */

                  )}

input.command: pnpm typecheck 2>&1 | tail -10 && pnpm check src/components/app-sidebar.tsx 2>&1 | tail -10

content.text:
Fixed. Root cause: with the `48px` collapsed sidebar, the header's `px-2` plus the button's inherited `px-2` (from `menuButtonVariants` size `md`, reinforced by the library's collapsed `px-2`) leaves only `16px` of button content box — and the base `overflow-hidden` on `SidebarMenuButton` was clipping the `32px` avatar's right edge.

Change in [apps/org-next/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx](apps/org-next/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx): added `collapsed && "overflow-visible"` to the button's className, which `twMerge` uses to drop the library's `overflow-hidden` in the same group. The avatar now renders fully (it visually extends across the narrow sidebar) and stays anchored at the same left position throughout the collapse transition — no position shift, no clipping.

Typecheck and Biome both still pass.

Got it — you want the avatar shifted left so it's not flush against the sidebar border. Easiest: drop the header-wrapper's horizontal padding in collapsed mode (keep vertical), so the avatar moves from a `16px` to `8px` offset and no longer needs `overflow-visible` to stay unclipped.

 The user is pointing out that the avatar is extending past the right edge of the sidebar when it's collapsed, so I need to reduce the left padding to keep it fully contained within the bounds. Adjusting the padding structure so the inner div uses conditional padding based on collapsed state — keeping vertical padding consistent while removing horizontal padding when collapsed, which avoids the height shift issue and gives a cleaner transition.

                    /* In collapsed mode the sidebar (48px) is narrower than the
                       avatar plus the button's own px-2 padding, so the library's

                       without shifting its left position. */

content.text:
Done. Changes in [apps/org-next/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx](apps/org-next/src/components/app-sidebar.tsx):

- Tenant-switcher wrapper is now `"border-sidebar-border py-2"` with `collapsed ? "px-0" : "border-b px-2"`. Collapsed state drops the `8px` horizontal padding, so the avatar starts at `8px` from the sidebar's left edge (the button's own `px-2`) and its right edge lands at `40px` — leaving `8px` of breathing room before the sidebar border.
- Dropped the `overflow-visible` override on the button. With the wrapper padding gone, the avatar fits entirely inside the button's content box, so the library's `overflow-hidden` is no longer a problem.

Avatar is still anchored to the left throughout (just at a slightly tighter `8px` offset when collapsed vs. `16px` when expanded), and the right side is no longer clipped. Typecheck and Biome pass.
